title: Audacity Capital 14% OFF
description: Audacity Capital, based in the United Kingdom and established in March 2012, is a CFD firm offering Instant, 2 Steps, and 1 Step program types. Account sizes range from $10,000 to $240,000. Tradable instruments include currencies, indices, and commodities. Verified by our [Intelligence Hub](/) for prop trading excellence.

timeline.event: The Wait Is Over, Introducing Ability One

seo_content:
Audacity Capital Technical Infrastructure

Audacity Capital, based in the United Kingdom, was established in March 2012, making it one of the longest-operating proprietary trading firms in the industry. It operates as a CFD prop firm offering three evaluation formats: the Ability Challenge (2-step), the Ability One (1-step), and the Funded Trader Program (Instant). Account sizes span from $7,500 to $240,000 across the combined program lineup. The firm introduced the Ability One program in late 2025, reducing prices further in early 2025. A coupon code (14th) delivers 14% off across programs.

The Ability Challenge 2-step program covers six account sizes: $10,000 at $76.50 (original $90.00), $15,000 at $110.50 (original $130.00), $30,000 at $195.50 (original $230.00), $60,000 at $272.00 (original $320.00), $120,000 at $459.00 (original $540.00), and $240,000 at $926.50 (original $1,090.00). The Ability One 1-step is available at $120,000 for $806.65 (original $949.00). The Funded Trader Program (Instant) offers three sizes: $7,500 at $296.10 (original $329.00), $15,000 at $584.10 (original $649.00), and $60,000 at $2,159.10 (original $2,399.00). Payments are accepted via Credit/Debit Card, Crypto, and PayPal.

Audacity Capital supports DXTrade and MT5 platforms. Instruments are categorized as CFD, covering currencies, indices, and commodities. Specific leverage ratios are not published. News trading entries are prohibited within 3 minutes before and after high-impact news releases and speeches, though existing positions may be held through news events. Weekend holding is permitted for Crypto positions. EAs are allowed provided they comply with all prohibited strategy rules; third-party signal services are not permitted.

The Ability Challenge profit split starts at 75% and increases to 85% when the trader achieves a profit of 10% or more, with a maximum split of 90%. The Funded Trader Program split ranges from 50% to 80% based on account size and the number of trading days, with eligibility beginning at 10% profit. Payouts are processed bi-weekly with a minimum withdrawal of $100. Payout methods include Bank Transfer, Crypto, PayPal, and Riseworks. Copy trading between the trader's own Audacity accounts is permitted; third-party copy services are prohibited.

Audacity Capital prohibits HFT, tick scalping, arbitrage, hedging across accounts, martingale and averaging-down strategies, group hedging, grid trading, pre-news one-sided positioning, exploitation of technical vulnerabilities, and automated bulk trade execution. The maximum individual open position size is 10 lots. The inactivity rule is notably lenient at 6 months — one of the longest inactivity windows in the industry — before an account is breached. The firm's March 2012 establishment and UK base give it a distinctly longer operational track record than most prop firms currently active in the market.
